toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Data Dictionary Web App - Roadmap

## Context

Build a demo-quality **Data Dictionary Web Application** that looks and feels like a polished SaaS product. The app lets businesses import dictionaries from multiple sources, define their own terms, search across all definitions, and use LLM to generate consolidated industry-aligned definitions. It will be hosted publicly so anyone with a link can use it.

The repo (`reg-scorecard`) is currently empty - just `.gitignore` and `README.md`.

---

## Recommended Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 14 (App Router) + TypeScript | SSR, file-based routing, deploys to Vercel in one click |
| **UI** | shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS | Professional SaaS look out of the box, dark mode, data tables |
| **Backend** | FastAPI (Python) | Matches repo's Python orientation, async, auto-generated API docs |
| **Database** | SQLite (MVP) → PostgreSQL (deploy) | Zero-config locally, single env var change to switch |
| **ORM** | SQLModel | Same models work for SQLite and PostgreSQL |
| **LLM** | Anthropic Claude API (Haiku for cost) | Excellent at synthesizing multiple definitions into one |
| **Dictionary APIs** | Free Dictionary API (no key needed) + Merriam-Webster (free tier, 1k/day) | Two complementary sources, both free |
| **PDF Processing** | PyMuPDF4LLM | Extracts PDF → Markdown in ~0.14s, feeds directly to LLM |
| **Hosting** | Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend + DB) | Free tiers, auto-deploy from git, shareable URLs |

---

## Data Model

```
Term (id, word, normalized_word, flag_status, is_custom, category, created_at, updated_at)
  └─< Definition (id, term_id, source_id, text, part_of_speech, is_llm_generated,
                   llm_prompt_context, is_primary, created_at)
Source (id, name, type[online|user_defined|pdf_import|llm_generated], url, is_active)
  └─< Definition
ImportJob (id, source_id, status, file_name, terms_imported, errors)
LLMRefinement (id, definition_id, user_prompt, previous_text, new_text, created_at)
```

---

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py, config.py, database.py
    models/    (term.py, flag.py, import_job.py)
    schemas/   (term.py, definition.py)
    routers/   (terms.py, definitions.py, sources.py, imports.py, llm.py)
    services/  (dictionary_service.py, llm_service.py, pdf_service.py, term_service.py)
    seed/      (sample_terms.json)
  tests/
  requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx          (sidebar nav, header with search)
    page.tsx            (dashboard)
    terms/page.tsx      (searchable term index)
    terms/[id]/page.tsx (term detail + definitions)
    import/page.tsx     (import sources)
    search/page.tsx     (global search)
  components/
    ui/                 (shadcn/ui components)
    term-card.tsx, definition-panel.tsx, source-badge.tsx,
    flag-indicator.tsx, search-bar.tsx, import-dropzone.tsx,
    llm-refinement-dialog.tsx, sidebar-nav.tsx
  lib/
    api.ts, types.ts

docker-compose.yml
```

---

## Phased Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation & MVP

**Goal:** Working app with term management, one dictionary source, and a polished UI shell.

**Backend:**
- Initialize FastAPI project with SQLModel + SQLite
- Create all data models (Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement)
- CRUD endpoints for Terms (`GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/terms`, `/api/terms/{id}`)
- CRUD endpoints for Definitions (`GET/POST /api/terms/{id}/definitions`)
- Integrate Free Dictionary API: `POST /api/lookup/{word}` fetches and stores definitions
- Seed database with 20-30 common business terms (process, procedure, framework, policy, governance, compliance, risk, control, etc.)
- CORS middleware

**Frontend:**
- Initialize Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- App shell: collapsible sidebar, header with global search, breadcrumbs
- **Dashboard page:** total terms, terms by status, recent terms, quick search
- **Terms Index page:** data table with search, sort, filter by status/category, pagination
- **Term Detail page:** term header, tabbed definitions by source, "Lookup" button
- **Add Term dialog:** form for custom business terms
- React Query for API calls, skeleton loading states, toast notifications
- Dark mode toggle, responsive layout, page transitions (Framer Motion)

**Deliverable:** Browse terms, click into details, add business terms, look up words from Free Dictionary API. Looks like a real SaaS app.

---

### Phase 2: Multi-Source & LLM Integration

**Goal:** Multiple dictionary sources, AI-powered consolidated definitions, refinement workflow.

**Backend:**
- Add Merriam-Webster API as second source
- Build pluggable `DictionaryService` abstraction (ABC with `lookup()` method)
- Aggregate lookup: query all sources concurrently via `asyncio.gather`
- Build `LLMService` with Claude integration:
  - `generate_consolidated_definition(word, definitions, context)` → synthesized definition
  - `refine_definition(word, current_def, user_prompt)` → refined definition
- Endpoints: `POST /api/terms/{id}/generate`, `POST /api/definitions/{id}/refine`
- LLMRefinement history tracking

**Frontend:**
- Definitions grouped by source with color-coded badges (blue=MW, green=Free Dict, purple=Business, amber=AI)
- "Generate AI Definition" button with loading animation
- **LLM Refinement Dialog:** shows current AI definition, text area for refinement prompt (with examples like "Emphasize regulatory compliance aspect"), accept/reject buttons
- Refinement history (collapsible)
- Sources management page (toggle sources on/off)
- Keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+K search, Cmd+N new term), Command palette

**Deliverable:** Look up words across multiple dictionaries, generate AI-consolidated definitions, refine them with natural language prompts.

---

### Phase 3: Import, Flagging & Advanced Search

**Goal:** Dictionary import, flagging/review system, full-text search.

**Backend:**
- Bulk import: `POST /api/import/terms` (CSV/JSON)
- Flagging: `PUT /api/terms/{id}/flag` (none, needs_review, approved, disputed, archived)
- Full-text search (SQLite FTS5 / PostgreSQL tsvector)
- Advanced search: `GET /api/search?q=...&sources=...&categories=...&flags=...`
- Export: `GET /api/export?format=csv|json`

**Frontend:**
- **Import page:** drag-and-drop for CSV/JSON, file preview, progress bar, results summary
- **Flagging UI:** status dropdown + note field on Term Detail, color-coded badges everywhere, filter chips
- **Enhanced Terms Index:** full-text search, faceted filters, bulk actions, column toggles, export button
- **Global Search page:** results grouped by match type, "Not found? Look it up" button
- Persistent filter state in URL (shareable filtered views)

**Deliverable:** Import business glossaries, flag terms for review, search across the entire dictionary with sophisticated filtering.

---

### Phase 4: PDF Import & Demo Polish

**Goal:** PDF document processing, definition extraction, demo-ready experience.

**Backend:**
- PyMuPDF4LLM integration for PDF → Markdown extraction
- PDF processing pipeline: upload → extract → send to Claude → parse extracted terms → create records
- Glossary detection heuristic (sections titled "Glossary", "Definitions", patterns like `"Term" means ...`)
- Async processing via FastAPI BackgroundTasks

**Frontend:**
- **PDF Import tab:** drag-and-drop for PDFs, processing status indicators ("Extracting text...", "Identifying terms..."), preview extracted terms before confirming
- PDF source references on Term Detail (which PDF, page number)
- "Suggested Terms" section for LLM-identified terms pending approval
- Guided onboarding tour (driver.js or react-joyride)
- Demo mode with pre-populated data and "Reset Demo" button

**Deliverable:** Complete demo app. Import PDFs, extract definitions, full lifecycle from raw document to curated AI-enhanced business dictionary.

---

## App-Like Features (Polish That Makes It Feel Real)

**Navigation:** Collapsible sidebar with icons, breadcrumbs, global Cmd+K search, user avatar placeholder

**Loading & Feedback:** Skeleton screens (not spinners), optimistic updates, toast notifications with undo, debounced search

**Visual Design:** Consistent color language per source type, status badges with dot indicators, card-based dashboard with hover effects, monospace font for terms, subtle gradient accent

**Animations:** Page transitions (fade + slide, 150ms), card hover lift, button press scale, accordion open/close, count-up on dashboard stats, typewriter effect for AI definitions

**Responsive:** Sidebar → icons → drawer on mobile, table → cards on mobile, full-screen search overlay on mobile

**Professional Signals:** "Powered by Claude" badge on AI definitions, source attribution on every definition, relative timestamps ("2 hours ago"), API source shown during lookups

---

## Key API Endpoints

```
GET/POST/PUT/DELETE  /api/terms, /api/terms/{id}
PUT                  /api/terms/{id}/flag
GET/POST             /api/terms/{id}/definitions
POST                 /api/lookup/{word}
GET/PUT              /api/sources, /api/sources/{id}
POST                 /api/terms/{id}/generate
POST                 /api/definitions/{id}/refine
GET                  /api/definitions/{id}/history
POST                 /api/import/terms
POST                 /api/import/pdf
GET                  /api/import/jobs, /api/import/jobs/{id}
GET                  /api/search?q=...
GET                  /api/export?format=csv|json
```

---

## Environment Variables

```
# Backend
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./data/dictionary.db
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
MERRIAM_WEBSTER_API_KEY=...
ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000,https://your-app.vercel.app

# Frontend
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
```

---

## Verification

1. **Local dev:** `docker-compose up` starts backend + DB; `npm run dev` starts frontend
2. **Smoke test flow:** Open dashboard → browse terms → click a term → hit "Lookup" → see external definitions appear → add custom term → generate AI definition → refine it → flag a term → import CSV → search
3. **Deploy test:** Push to main → Vercel auto-deploys frontend → Railway auto-deploys backend → visit public URL → complete smoke test flow
4. **API docs:** Visit `/docs` on backend URL for auto-generated Swagger UI

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Dictionary API rate limits/downtime | Cache responses in DB; serve from cache after first fetch |
| LLM costs | Use Claude Haiku; cache generated definitions; rate limit |
| PDF extraction quality | Show extracted text for review before import; allow manual edits |
| Cold starts on free tiers | Health check endpoint; frontend shows "Connecting..." gracefully |
| CORS between Vercel and Railway | Configure FastAPI CORS with explicit Vercel domain |

lines: | **Backend** | FastAPI (Python) | Matches repo's Python orientation, async, auto-generated API docs |
| **Database** | SQLite (MVP) → PostgreSQL (deploy) | Zero-config locally, single env var change to switch |
| **ORM** | SQLModel | Same models work for SQLite and PostgreSQL |
-| **LLM** | Anthropic Claude API (Haiku for cost) | Excellent at synthesizing multiple definitions into one |
+| **LLM** | Ollama (local) / Hugging Face Inference API (free tier) | No API key needed; Mistral-7B or similar for definition synthesis |
| **Dictionary APIs** | Free Dictionary API (no key needed) + Merriam-Webster (free tier, 1k/day) | Two complementary sources, both free |
| **PDF Processing** | PyMuPDF4LLM | Extracts PDF → Markdown in ~0.14s, feeds directly to LLM |
| **Hosting** | Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend + DB) | Free tiers, auto-deploy from git, shareable URLs |

from sqlmodel import SQLModel
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, create_async_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

from app.config import settings

engine = create_async_engine(settings.database_url, echo=False)

async_session = sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)


async def init_db():
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(SQLModel.metadata.create_all)


async def get_session() -> AsyncSession:
    async with async_session() as session:
        yield session

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

from sqlmodel import Field, Relationship, SQLModel


class FlagStatus(str, Enum):
    none = "none"
    needs_review = "needs_review"
    approved = "approved"
    disputed = "disputed"
    archived = "archived"


class SourceType(str, Enum):
    online = "online"
    user_defined = "user_defined"
    pdf_import = "pdf_import"
    llm_generated = "llm_generated"


class ImportStatus(str, Enum):
    pending = "pending"
    processing = "processing"
    completed = "completed"
    failed = "failed"


class Source(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)
    name: str = Field(index=True)
    type: SourceType = Field(default=SourceType.online)
    url: Optional[str] = None
    is_active: bool = Field(default=True)
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    definitions: list["Definition"] = Relationship(back_populates="source")


class Term(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)
    word: str = Field(index=True)
    normalized_word: str = Field(index=True)
    flag_status: FlagStatus = Field(default=FlagStatus.none)
    is_custom: bool = Field(default=False)
    category: Optional[str] = None
    notes: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    definitions: list["Definition"] = Relationship(back_populates="term")


class Definition(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)
    term_id: int = Field(foreign_key="term.id", index=True)
    source_id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, foreign_key="source.id", index=True)
    text: str
    part_of_speech: Optional[str] = None
    example: Optional[str] = None
    is_llm_generated: bool = Field(default=False)
    llm_prompt_context: Optional[str] = None
    is_primary: bool = Field(default=False)
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    term: Optional[Term] = Relationship(back_populates="definitions")
    source: Optional[Source] = Relationship(back_populates="definitions")


class ImportJob(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)
    source_id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, foreign_key="source.id")
    status: ImportStatus = Field(default=ImportStatus.pending)
    file_name: Optional[str] = None
    terms_imported: int = Field(default=0)
    errors: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))


class LLMRefinement(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)
    definition_id: int = Field(foreign_key="definition.id")
    user_prompt: str
    previous_text: str

from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

from sqlmodel import Field, Relationship, SQLModel


class FlagStatus(str, Enum):
    none = "none"
    needs_review = "needs_review"
    approved = "approved"
    disputed = "disputed"
    archived = "archived"


class SourceType(str, Enum):
    online = "online"
    user_defined = "user_defined"
    pdf_import = "pdf_import"
    llm_generated = "llm_generated"


class ImportStatus(str, Enum):
    pending = "pending"
    processing = "processing"
    completed = "completed"
    failed = "failed"


class Source(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, primary_key=True)

    is_active: bool = Field(default=True)
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    definitions: list["Definition"] = Relationship(back_populates="source")

    normalized_word: str = Field(index=True)
    flag_status: FlagStatus = Field(default=FlagStatus.none)

    notes: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

class Definition(SQLModel, table=True):

    term: Optional[Term] = Relationship(back_populates="definitions")

    terms_imported: int = Field(default=0)
    errors: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))


class LLMRefinement(SQLModel, table=True):

    definition_id: int = Field(foreign_key="definition.id")
    user_prompt: str
    previous_text: str
    new_text: str

gitDiff.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+import httpx
+
+from app.config import settings
+
+
+async def lookup_free_dictionary(word: str) -> list[dict]:
+    """Fetch definitions from the Free Dictionary API (no key required)."""
+    url = f"{settings.free_dictionary_api_url}/{word}"
+    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
+        resp = await client.get(url)
+
+    if resp.status_code != 200:
+        return []
+
+    results = []
+    for entry in resp.json():
+        for meaning in entry.get("meanings", []):
+            pos = meaning.get("partOfSpeech", "")
+            for defn in meaning.get("definitions", []):
+                results.append(
+                    {
+                        "text": defn.get("definition", ""),
+                        "part_of_speech": pos,
+                        "example": defn.get("example"),
+                    }
+                )
+    return results
+
+
+async def lookup_merriam_webster(word: str) -> list[dict]:
+    """Fetch definitions from the Merriam-Webster API (requires API key)."""
+    if not settings.merriam_webster_api_key:
+        return []
+
+    url = f"{settings.merriam_webster_api_url}/{word}?key={settings.merriam_webster_api_key}"
+    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
+        resp = await client.get(url)
+
+    if resp.status_code != 200:
+        return []
+
+    data = resp.json()
+    if not data or not isinstance(data[0], dict):
+        return []
+
+    results = []
+    for entry in data:
+        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
+            continue
+        pos = entry.get("fl", "")
+        for shortdef in entry.get("shortdef", []):
+            results.append(
+                {
+                    "text": shortdef,
+                    "part_of_speech": pos,
+                    "example": None,
+                }
+            )
+    return results
+
+
+DICTIONARY_SOURCES = {
+    "Free Dictionary": lookup_free_dictionary,
+    "Merriam-Webster": lookup_merriam_webster,
+}

input.content:
from datetime import datetime, timezone

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import col, func, select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, FlagStatus, Source, Term
from app.schemas.term import (
    DashboardStats,
    DefinitionCreate,
    DefinitionRead,
    FlagUpdate,
    TermCreate,
    TermRead,
    TermSummary,
    TermUpdate,
)

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/terms", tags=["terms"])


@router.get("", response_model=list[TermSummary])
async def list_terms(
    q: str = Query("", description="Search query"),
    flag_status: FlagStatus | None = None,
    category: str | None = None,
    is_custom: bool | None = None,
    skip: int = 0,
    limit: int = 50,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
):
    query = select(Term)

    if q:
        query = query.where(col(Term.normalized_word).contains(q.lower()))
    if flag_status:
        query = query.where(Term.flag_status == flag_status)
    if category:
        query = query.where(Term.category == category)
    if is_custom is not None:
        query = query.where(Term.is_custom == is_custom)

    query = query.order_by(col(Term.word)).offset(skip).limit(limit)
    result = await session.execute(query)
    terms = result.scalars().all()

    summaries = []
    for term in terms:
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        summaries.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return summaries


@router.get("/stats", response_model=DashboardStats)
async def get_stats(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    total = (await session.execute(select(func.count(Term.id)))).scalar() or 0
    approved = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.approved
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    needs_review = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.needs_review
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    custom = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(Term.is_custom == True)  # noqa: E712
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_defs = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Definition.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_sources = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Source.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0

    recent_q = select(Term).order_by(col(Term.created_at).desc()).limit(5)
    recent_result = await session.execute(recent_q)
    recent_terms = []
    for term in recent_result.scalars().all():
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        recent_terms.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return DashboardStats(
        total_terms=total,
        approved_terms=approved,
        needs_review_terms=needs_review,
        custom_terms=custom,
        total_definitions=total_defs,
        total_sources=total_sources,
        recent_terms=recent_terms,
    )


@router.post("", response_model=TermRead, status_code=201)
async def create_term(
    data: TermCreate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    term = Term(
        word=data.word,
        normalized_word=data.word.lower().strip(),
        is_custom=True,
        category=data.category,
        notes=data.notes,
    )
    session.add(term)
    await session.flush()

    if data.definition:
        user_source = (
            await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.name == "User Defined")
            )
        ).scalars().first()
        if not user_source:
            user_source = Source(name="User Defined", type="user_defined")
            session.add(user_source)
            await session.flush()

        definition = Definition(
            term_id=term.id,
            source_id=user_source.id,
            text=data.definition,
            is_primary=True,
        )
        session.add(definition)

    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    result = await session.execute(
        select(Term).where(Term.id == term.id)
    )
    term = result.scalars().first()
    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.get("/{term_id}", response_model=TermRead)
async def get_term(term_id: int, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.put("/{term_id}", response_model=TermRead)
async def update_term(
    term_id: int, data: TermUpdate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    if data.word is not None:
        term.word = data.word
        term.normalized_word = data.word.lower().strip()
    if data.category is not None:
        term.category = data.category
    if data.notes is not None:
        term.notes = data.notes
    if data.flag_status is not None:
        term.flag_status = data.flag_status

    term.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    session.add(term)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.delete("/{term_id}", status_code=204)
async def delete_term(term_id: int, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    defs = (
        await session.execute(
            select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        )
    ).scalars().all()
    for d in defs:
        await session.delete(d)

    await session.delete(term)
    await session.commit()


@router.put("/{term_id}/flag", response_model=TermRead)
async def flag_term(
    term_id: int, data: FlagUpdate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    term.flag_status = data.flag_status
    if data.notes is not None:
        term.notes = data.notes
    term.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    session.add(term)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.post("/{term_id}/definitions", response_model=DefinitionRead, status_code=201)
async def add_definition(
    term_id: int,
    data: DefinitionCreate,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    source = None
    if data.source_id:
        source_result = await session.execute(
            select(Source).where(Source.id == data.source_id)
        )
        source = source_result.scalars().first()

    definition = Definition(
        term_id=term_id,
        source_id=data.source_id,
        text=data.text,
        part_of_speech=data.part_of_speech,
        example=data.example,
        is_primary=data.is_primary,
    )
    session.add(definition)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(definition)
    definition.source = source
    return definition

from datetime import datetime, timezone

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import col, func, select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, FlagStatus, Source, Term
from app.schemas.term import (
    DashboardStats,
    DefinitionCreate,
    DefinitionRead,
    FlagUpdate,
    TermCreate,
    TermRead,
    TermSummary,
    TermUpdate,
)

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/terms", tags=["terms"])


@router.get("", response_model=list[TermSummary])
async def list_terms(
    q: str = Query("", description="Search query"),
    flag_status: FlagStatus | None = None,
    category: str | None = None,
    is_custom: bool | None = None,
    skip: int = 0,
    limit: int = 50,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
):
    query = select(Term)

    if q:
        query = query.where(col(Term.normalized_word).contains(q.lower()))
    if flag_status:
        query = query.where(Term.flag_status == flag_status)
    if category:
        query = query.where(Term.category == category)
    if is_custom is not None:
        query = query.where(Term.is_custom == is_custom)

    query = query.order_by(col(Term.word)).offset(skip).limit(limit)
    result = await session.execute(query)
    terms = result.scalars().all()

    summaries = []
    for term in terms:
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        summaries.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return summaries


@router.get("/stats", response_model=DashboardStats)
async def get_stats(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    total = (await session.execute(select(func.count(Term.id)))).scalar() or 0
    approved = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.approved
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    needs_review = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.needs_review
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    custom = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(Term.is_custom == True)  # noqa: E712
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_defs = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Definition.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_sources = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Source.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0

    recent_q = select(Term).order_by(col(Term.created_at).desc()).limit(5)
    recent_result = await session.execute(recent_q)
    recent_terms = []
    for term in recent_result.scalars().all():
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        recent_terms.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return DashboardStats(
        total_terms=total,
        approved_terms=approved,
        needs_review_terms=needs_review,
        custom_terms=custom,
        total_definitions=total_defs,
        total_sources=total_sources,
        recent_terms=recent_terms,
    )


@router.post("", response_model=TermRead, status_code=201)
async def create_term(
    data: TermCreate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    term = Term(
        word=data.word,
        normalized_word=data.word.lower().strip(),
        is_custom=True,
        category=data.category,
        notes=data.notes,
    )
    session.add(term)
    await session.flush()

    if data.definition:
        user_source = (
            await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.name == "User Defined")
            )
        ).scalars().first()
        if not user_source:
            user_source = Source(name="User Defined", type="user_defined")
            session.add(user_source)
            await session.flush()

        definition = Definition(
            term_id=term.id,
            source_id=user_source.id,
            text=data.definition,
            is_primary=True,
        )
        session.add(definition)

    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    result = await session.execute(
        select(Term).where(Term.id == term.id)
    )
    term = result.scalars().first()
    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.get("/{term_id}", response_model=TermRead)
async def get_term(term_id: int, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.put("/{term_id}", response_model=TermRead)
async def update_term(
    term_id: int, data: TermUpdate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    if data.word is not None:
        term.word = data.word
        term.normalized_word = data.word.lower().strip()
    if data.category is not None:
        term.category = data.category
    if data.notes is not None:
        term.notes = data.notes
    if data.flag_status is not None:
        term.flag_status = data.flag_status

    term.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    session.add(term)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.delete("/{term_id}", status_code=204)
async def delete_term(term_id: int, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    defs = (
        await session.execute(
            select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        )
    ).scalars().all()
    for d in defs:
        await session.delete(d)

    await session.delete(term)
    await session.commit()


@router.put("/{term_id}/flag", response_model=TermRead)
async def flag_term(
    term_id: int, data: FlagUpdate, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    term.flag_status = data.flag_status
    if data.notes is not None:
        term.notes = data.notes
    term.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    session.add(term)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(term)

    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    definitions = defs_result.scalars().all()
    for d in definitions:
        if d.source_id:
            source_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            d.source = source_result.scalars().first()

    term.definitions = list(definitions)
    return term


@router.post("/{term_id}/definitions", response_model=DefinitionRead, status_code=201)
async def add_definition(
    term_id: int,
    data: DefinitionCreate,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
):
    result = await session.execute(select(Term).where(Term.id == term_id))
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Term not found")

    source = None
    if data.source_id:
        source_result = await session.execute(
            select(Source).where(Source.id == data.source_id)
        )
        source = source_result.scalars().first()

    definition = Definition(
        term_id=term_id,
        source_id=data.source_id,
        text=data.text,
        part_of_speech=data.part_of_speech,
        example=data.example,
        is_primary=data.is_primary,
    )
    session.add(definition)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(definition)
    definition.source = source
    return definition

import asyncio

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, Source, SourceType, Term
from app.schemas.term import DefinitionRead, LookupResult, SourceRead
from app.services.dictionary_service import DICTIONARY_SOURCES

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/lookup", tags=["lookup"])


@router.post("/{word}", response_model=LookupResult)
async def lookup_word(word: str, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    """Look up a word across all active dictionary sources and store new definitions."""
    normalized = word.lower().strip()

    # Find or create the term
    result = await session.execute(
        select(Term).where(Term.normalized_word == normalized)
    )
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        term = Term(word=word, normalized_word=normalized)
        session.add(term)
        await session.flush()

    # Query all dictionary sources concurrently
    tasks = {}
    for source_name, lookup_fn in DICTIONARY_SOURCES.items():
        tasks[source_name] = asyncio.create_task(lookup_fn(word))

    new_count = 0
    sources_searched = []

    for source_name, task in tasks.items():
        try:
            definitions = await task
        except Exception:
            continue

        sources_searched.append(source_name)

        if not definitions:
            continue

        # Get or create the source record
        source_result = await session.execute(
            select(Source).where(Source.name == source_name)
        )
        source = source_result.scalars().first()
        if not source:
            source = Source(name=source_name, type=SourceType.online)
            session.add(source)
            await session.flush()

        # Check existing definitions from this source for this term
        existing = await session.execute(
            select(Definition).where(
                Definition.term_id == term.id,
                Definition.source_id == source.id,
            )
        )
        existing_texts = {d.text for d in existing.scalars().all()}

        for defn in definitions:
            if defn["text"] not in existing_texts:
                definition = Definition(
                    term_id=term.id,
                    source_id=source.id,
                    text=defn["text"],
                    part_of_speech=defn.get("part_of_speech"),
                    example=defn.get("example"),
                )
                session.add(definition)
                new_count += 1

    await session.commit()

    # Return all definitions for this term
    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    all_defs = defs_result.scalars().all()
    def_reads = []
    for d in all_defs:
        source = None
        if d.source_id:
            src_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            source = src_result.scalars().first()
        def_reads.append(
            DefinitionRead(
                id=d.id,
                term_id=d.term_id,
                source_id=d.source_id,
                text=d.text,
                part_of_speech=d.part_of_speech,
                example=d.example,
                is_llm_generated=d.is_llm_generated,
                is_primary=d.is_primary,
                source=SourceRead.model_validate(source) if source else None,
                created_at=d.created_at,
            )
        )

    return LookupResult(
        word=word,
        definitions=def_reads,
        sources_searched=sources_searched,
        new_definitions_added=new_count,
    )

import asyncio

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, Source, SourceType, Term
from app.schemas.term import DefinitionRead, LookupResult, SourceRead
from app.services.dictionary_service import DICTIONARY_SOURCES

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/lookup", tags=["lookup"])


@router.post("/{word}", response_model=LookupResult)
async def lookup_word(word: str, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    """Look up a word across all active dictionary sources and store new definitions."""
    normalized = word.lower().strip()

    # Find or create the term
    result = await session.execute(
        select(Term).where(Term.normalized_word == normalized)
    )
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        term = Term(word=word, normalized_word=normalized)
        session.add(term)
        await session.flush()

    # Query all dictionary sources concurrently
    tasks = {}
    for source_name, lookup_fn in DICTIONARY_SOURCES.items():
        tasks[source_name] = asyncio.create_task(lookup_fn(word))

    new_count = 0
    sources_searched = []

    for source_name, task in tasks.items():
        try:
            definitions = await task
        except Exception:
            continue

        sources_searched.append(source_name)

        if not definitions:
            continue

        # Get or create the source record
        source_result = await session.execute(
            select(Source).where(Source.name == source_name)
        )
        source = source_result.scalars().first()
        if not source:
            source = Source(name=source_name, type=SourceType.online)
            session.add(source)
            await session.flush()

        # Check existing definitions from this source for this term
        existing = await session.execute(
            select(Definition).where(
                Definition.term_id == term.id,
                Definition.source_id == source.id,
            )
        )
        existing_texts = {d.text for d in existing.scalars().all()}

        for defn in definitions:
            if defn["text"] not in existing_texts:
                definition = Definition(
                    term_id=term.id,
                    source_id=source.id,
                    text=defn["text"],
                    part_of_speech=defn.get("part_of_speech"),
                    example=defn.get("example"),
                )
                session.add(definition)
                new_count += 1

    await session.commit()

    # Return all definitions for this term
    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    all_defs = defs_result.scalars().all()
    def_reads = []
    for d in all_defs:
        source = None
        if d.source_id:
            src_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            source = src_result.scalars().first()
        def_reads.append(
            DefinitionRead(
                id=d.id,
                term_id=d.term_id,
                source_id=d.source_id,
                text=d.text,
                part_of_speech=d.part_of_speech,
                example=d.example,
                is_llm_generated=d.is_llm_generated,
                is_primary=d.is_primary,
                source=SourceRead.model_validate(source) if source else None,
                created_at=d.created_at,
            )
        )

    return LookupResult(
        word=word,
        definitions=def_reads,
        sources_searched=sources_searched,
        new_definitions_added=new_count,
    )

input.content:
import json
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.config import settings
from app.database import async_session, engine, init_db
from app.models.term import Definition, Source, SourceType, Term
from app.routers import lookup, sources, terms


async def seed_database():
    """Seed the database with sample terms if empty."""
    async with async_session() as session:
        result = await session.execute(select(Term))
        if result.scalars().first() is not None:
            return

        # Create the "User Defined" source
        user_source = Source(name="User Defined", type=SourceType.user_defined)
        session.add(user_source)

        # Create the dictionary sources
        free_dict_source = Source(
            name="Free Dictionary",
            type=SourceType.online,
            url="https://dictionaryapi.dev/",
        )
        session.add(free_dict_source)

        mw_source = Source(
            name="Merriam-Webster",
            type=SourceType.online,
            url="https://dictionaryapi.com/",
        )
        session.add(mw_source)

        await session.flush()

        seed_file = Path(__file__).parent / "seed" / "sample_terms.json"
        with open(seed_file) as f:
            sample_terms = json.load(f)

        for item in sample_terms:
            term = Term(
                word=item["word"],
                normalized_word=item["word"].lower().strip(),
                is_custom=True,
                category=item.get("category"),
                flag_status="needs_review",
            )
            session.add(term)
            await session.flush()

            definition = Definition(
                term_id=term.id,
                source_id=user_source.id,
                text=item["definition"],
                is_primary=True,
            )
            session.add(definition)

        await session.commit()
        print(f"Seeded {len(sample_terms)} terms")


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Create data directory
    Path("data").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    await init_db()
    await seed_database()
    yield
    await engine.dispose()


app = FastAPI(
    title="Data Dictionary API",
    description="A comprehensive data dictionary management tool",
    version="0.1.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.allowed_origins.split(","),
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(terms.router)
app.include_router(lookup.router)
app.include_router(sources.router)


@app.get("/api/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}

content.text: Now let me update the README with comprehensive setup instructions.

input.content:
# DataDict - Data Dictionary Web App

A comprehensive data dictionary management tool that aggregates definitions from multiple sources, supports business-specific terminology, and provides AI-powered definition synthesis.

## Features

- **Multi-source dictionary lookup** - Search terms across Free Dictionary API, Merriam-Webster, and more
- **Business data dictionary** - Define and manage custom business terms with categories and flags
- **Searchable term index** - Browse, filter, and search all terms with status tracking
- **Definition aggregation** - View all definitions for a term grouped by source
- **Flagging system** - Flag terms as approved, needs review, disputed, or archived
- **Dark mode** - Full dark/light theme support
- **Responsive design** - Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Backend | FastAPI, Python 3.12, SQLModel, SQLite |
| State | TanStack React Query |
| UI | Custom components (shadcn/ui-inspired) |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- Node.js 18+
- npm

### Backend

```bash
cd backend
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

The API will be available at http://localhost:8000. Visit http://localhost:8000/docs for the interactive API docs.

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

The app will be available at http://localhost:3000.

### Docker Compose

```bash
docker-compose up
```

Both services will start - frontend at http://localhost:3000, backend at http://localhost:8000.

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
    config.py            # Environment configuration
    database.py          # Database engine and session
    models/term.py       # SQLModel data models
    schemas/term.py      # Pydantic API schemas
    routers/
      terms.py           # Term CRUD + flagging endpoints
      lookup.py          # External dictionary lookup
      sources.py         # Dictionary source management
    services/
      dictionary_service.py  # External API integrations
    seed/
      sample_terms.json  # Initial business terms
  requirements.txt
  Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx           # Root layout with sidebar
    page.tsx             # Dashboard
    terms/page.tsx       # Terms index
    terms/[id]/page.tsx  # Term detail
    search/page.tsx      # Global search + lookup
  components/
    ui/                  # Reusable UI components
    sidebar-nav.tsx      # Navigation sidebar
    add-term-dialog.tsx  # New term form
    flag-badge.tsx       # Status badges
    source-badge.tsx     # Source type badges
  lib/
    api.ts               # API client functions
    types.ts             # TypeScript type definitions
```

## Roadmap

See [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) for the full phased development roadmap.

+
+### Docker Compose
+
+```bash
+docker-compose up
+```
+
+Both services will start - frontend at http://localhost:3000, backend at http://localhost:8000.
+
+## Project Structure
+
+```
+backend/
+  app/
+    main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
+    config.py            # Environment configuration
+    database.py          # Database engine and session
+    models/term.py       # SQLModel data models
+    schemas/term.py      # Pydantic API schemas
+    routers/
+      terms.py           # Term CRUD + flagging endpoints
+      lookup.py          # External dictionary lookup
+      sources.py         # Dictionary source management
+    services/
+      dictionary_service.py  # External API integrations
+    seed/
+      sample_terms.json  # Initial business terms
+  requirements.txt
+  Dockerfile
+
+frontend/
+  app/
+    layout.tsx           # Root layout with sidebar
+    page.tsx             # Dashboard
+    terms/page.tsx       # Terms index
+    terms/[id]/page.tsx  # Term detail
+    search/page.tsx      # Global search + lookup
+  components/
+    ui/                  # Reusable UI components
+    sidebar-nav.tsx      # Navigation sidebar
+    add-term-dialog.tsx  # New term form
+    flag-badge.tsx       # Status badges
+    source-badge.tsx     # Source type badges
+  lib/
+    api.ts               # API client functions
+    types.ts             # TypeScript type definitions
+```
+
+## Roadmap
+
+See [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) for the full phased development roadmap.

lines: \ No newline at end of file
+A comprehensive data dictionary management tool that aggregates definitions from multiple sources, supports business-specific terminology, and provides AI-powered definition synthesis.
+- **Multi-source dictionary lookup** - Search terms across Free Dictionary API, Merriam-Webster, and more
+- **Business data dictionary** - Define and manage custom business terms with categories and flags
+- **Searchable term index** - Browse, filter, and search all terms with status tracking
+- **Definition aggregation** - View all definitions for a term grouped by source
+- **Flagging system** - Flag terms as approved, needs review, disputed, or archived
+- **Responsive design** - Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile
+| Frontend | Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 |
+The API will be available at http://localhost:8000. Visit http://localhost:8000/docs for the interactive API docs.
+The app will be available at http://localhost:3000.
+Both services will start - frontend at http://localhost:3000, backend at http://localhost:8000.
+See [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) for the full phased development roadmap.

# Data Dictionary Web App - Roadmap

## Context

Build a demo-quality **Data Dictionary Web Application** that looks and feels like a polished SaaS product. The app lets businesses import dictionaries from multiple sources, define their own terms, search across all definitions, and use LLM to generate consolidated industry-aligned definitions. It will be hosted publicly so anyone with a link can use it.

The repo (`reg-scorecard`) is currently empty - just `.gitignore` and `README.md`.

---

## Recommended Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 14 (App Router) + TypeScript | SSR, file-based routing, deploys to Vercel in one click |
| **UI** | shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS | Professional SaaS look out of the box, dark mode, data tables |
| **Backend** | FastAPI (Python) | Matches repo's Python orientation, async, auto-generated API docs |
| **Database** | SQLite (MVP) → PostgreSQL (deploy) | Zero-config locally, single env var change to switch |
| **ORM** | SQLModel | Same models work for SQLite and PostgreSQL |
| **LLM** | Ollama (local) / Hugging Face Inference API (free tier) | No API key needed; Mistral-7B or similar for definition synthesis |
| **Dictionary APIs** | Free Dictionary API (no key needed) + Merriam-Webster (free tier, 1k/day) | Two complementary sources, both free |
| **PDF Processing** | PyMuPDF4LLM | Extracts PDF → Markdown in ~0.14s, feeds directly to LLM |
| **Hosting** | Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend + DB) | Free tiers, auto-deploy from git, shareable URLs |

---

## Data Model

```
Term (id, word, normalized_word, flag_status, is_custom, category, created_at, updated_at)
  └─< Definition (id, term_id, source_id, text, part_of_speech, is_llm_generated,
                   llm_prompt_context, is_primary, created_at)
Source (id, name, type[online|user_defined|pdf_import|llm_generated], url, is_active)
  └─< Definition
ImportJob (id, source_id, status, file_name, terms_imported, errors)
LLMRefinement (id, definition_id, user_prompt, previous_text, new_text, created_at)
```

---

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py, config.py, database.py
    models/    (term.py, flag.py, import_job.py)
    schemas/   (term.py, definition.py)
    routers/   (terms.py, definitions.py, sources.py, imports.py, llm.py)
    services/  (dictionary_service.py, llm_service.py, pdf_service.py, term_service.py)
    seed/      (sample_terms.json)
  tests/
  requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx          (sidebar nav, header with search)
    page.tsx            (dashboard)
    terms/page.tsx      (searchable term index)
    terms/[id]/page.tsx (term detail + definitions)
    import/page.tsx     (import sources)
    search/page.tsx     (global search)
  components/
    ui/                 (shadcn/ui components)
    term-card.tsx, definition-panel.tsx, source-badge.tsx,
    flag-indicator.tsx, search-bar.tsx, import-dropzone.tsx,
    llm-refinement-dialog.tsx, sidebar-nav.tsx
  lib/
    api.ts, types.ts

docker-compose.yml
```

---

## Phased Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation & MVP

**Goal:** Working app with term management, one dictionary source, and a polished UI shell.

**Backend:**
- Initialize FastAPI project with SQLModel + SQLite
- Create all data models (Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement)
- CRUD endpoints for Terms (`GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/terms`, `/api/terms/{id}`)
- CRUD endpoints for Definitions (`GET/POST /api/terms/{id}/definitions`)
- Integrate Free Dictionary API: `POST /api/lookup/{word}` fetches and stores definitions
- Seed database with 20-30 common business terms (process, procedure, framework, policy, governance, compliance, risk, control, etc.)
- CORS middleware

**Frontend:**
- Initialize Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- App shell: collapsible sidebar, header with global search, breadcrumbs
- **Dashboard page:** total terms, terms by status, recent terms, quick search
- **Terms Index page:** data table with search, sort, filter by status/category, pagination
- **Term Detail page:** term header, tabbed definitions by source, "Lookup" button
- **Add Term dialog:** form for custom business terms
- React Query for API calls, skeleton loading states, toast notifications
- Dark mode toggle, responsive layout, page transitions (Framer Motion)

**Deliverable:** Browse terms, click into details, add business terms, look up words from Free Dictionary API. Looks like a real SaaS app.

---

### Phase 2: Multi-Source & LLM Integration

**Goal:** Multiple dictionary sources, AI-powered consolidated definitions, refinement workflow.

**Backend:**
- Add Merriam-Webster API as second source
- Build pluggable `DictionaryService` abstraction (ABC with `lookup()` method)
- Aggregate lookup: query all sources concurrently via `asyncio.gather`
- Build `LLMService` with Claude integration:
  - `generate_consolidated_definition(word, definitions, context)` → synthesized definition
  - `refine_definition(word, current_def, user_prompt)` → refined definition
- Endpoints: `POST /api/terms/{id}/generate`, `POST /api/definitions/{id}/refine`
- LLMRefinement history tracking

**Frontend:**
- Definitions grouped by source with color-coded badges (blue=MW, green=Free Dict, purple=Business, amber=AI)
- "Generate AI Definition" button with loading animation
- **LLM Refinement Dialog:** shows current AI definition, text area for refinement prompt (with examples like "Emphasize regulatory compliance aspect"), accept/reject buttons
- Refinement history (collapsible)
- Sources management page (toggle sources on/off)
- Keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+K search, Cmd+N new term), Command palette

**Deliverable:** Look up words across multiple dictionaries, generate AI-consolidated definitions, refine them with natural language prompts.

---

### Phase 3: Import, Flagging & Advanced Search

**Goal:** Dictionary import, flagging/review system, full-text search.

**Backend:**
- Bulk import: `POST /api/import/terms` (CSV/JSON)
- Flagging: `PUT /api/terms/{id}/flag` (none, needs_review, approved, disputed, archived)
- Full-text search (SQLite FTS5 / PostgreSQL tsvector)
- Advanced search: `GET /api/search?q=...&sources=...&categories=...&flags=...`
- Export: `GET /api/export?format=csv|json`

**Frontend:**
- **Import page:** drag-and-drop for CSV/JSON, file preview, progress bar, results summary
- **Flagging UI:** status dropdown + note field on Term Detail, color-coded badges everywhere, filter chips
- **Enhanced Terms Index:** full-text search, faceted filters, bulk actions, column toggles, export button
- **Global Search page:** results grouped by match type, "Not found? Look it up" button
- Persistent filter state in URL (shareable filtered views)

**Deliverable:** Import business glossaries, flag terms for review, search across the entire dictionary with sophisticated filtering.

---

### Phase 4: PDF Import & Demo Polish

**Goal:** PDF document processing, definition extraction, demo-ready experience.

**Backend:**
- PyMuPDF4LLM integration for PDF → Markdown extraction
- PDF processing pipeline: upload → extract → send to Claude → parse extracted terms → create records
- Glossary detection heuristic (sections titled "Glossary", "Definitions", patterns like `"Term" means ...`)
- Async processing via FastAPI BackgroundTasks

**Frontend:**
- **PDF Import tab:** drag-and-drop for PDFs, processing status indicators ("Extracting text...", "Identifying terms..."), preview extracted terms before confirming
- PDF source references on Term Detail (which PDF, page number)
- "Suggested Terms" section for LLM-identified terms pending approval
- Guided onboarding tour (driver.js or react-joyride)
- Demo mode with pre-populated data and "Reset Demo" button

**Deliverable:** Complete demo app. Import PDFs, extract definitions, full lifecycle from raw document to curated AI-enhanced business dictionary.

---

## App-Like Features (Polish That Makes It Feel Real)

**Navigation:** Collapsible sidebar with icons, breadcrumbs, global Cmd+K search, user avatar placeholder

**Loading & Feedback:** Skeleton screens (not spinners), optimistic updates, toast notifications with undo, debounced search

**Visual Design:** Consistent color language per source type, status badges with dot indicators, card-based dashboard with hover effects, monospace font for terms, subtle gradient accent

**Animations:** Page transitions (fade + slide, 150ms), card hover lift, button press scale, accordion open/close, count-up on dashboard stats, typewriter effect for AI definitions

**Responsive:** Sidebar → icons → drawer on mobile, table → cards on mobile, full-screen search overlay on mobile

**Professional Signals:** "Powered by Claude" badge on AI definitions, source attribution on every definition, relative timestamps ("2 hours ago"), API source shown during lookups

---

## Key API Endpoints

```
GET/POST/PUT/DELETE  /api/terms, /api/terms/{id}
PUT                  /api/terms/{id}/flag
GET/POST             /api/terms/{id}/definitions
POST                 /api/lookup/{word}
GET/PUT              /api/sources, /api/sources/{id}
POST                 /api/terms/{id}/generate
POST                 /api/definitions/{id}/refine
GET                  /api/definitions/{id}/history
POST                 /api/import/terms
POST                 /api/import/pdf
GET                  /api/import/jobs, /api/import/jobs/{id}
GET                  /api/search?q=...
GET                  /api/export?format=csv|json
```

---

## Environment Variables

```
# Backend
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./data/dictionary.db
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
MERRIAM_WEBSTER_API_KEY=...
ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000,https://your-app.vercel.app

# Frontend
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
```

---

## Verification

1. **Local dev:** `docker-compose up` starts backend + DB; `npm run dev` starts frontend
2. **Smoke test flow:** Open dashboard → browse terms → click a term → hit "Lookup" → see external definitions appear → add custom term → generate AI definition → refine it → flag a term → import CSV → search
3. **Deploy test:** Push to main → Vercel auto-deploys frontend → Railway auto-deploys backend → visit public URL → complete smoke test flow
4. **API docs:** Visit `/docs` on backend URL for auto-generated Swagger UI

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Dictionary API rate limits/downtime | Cache responses in DB; serve from cache after first fetch |
| LLM costs | Use Claude Haiku; cache generated definitions; rate limit |
| PDF extraction quality | Show extracted text for review before import; allow manual edits |
| Cold starts on free tiers | Health check endpoint; frontend shows "Connecting..." gracefully |
| CORS between Vercel and Railway | Configure FastAPI CORS with explicit Vercel domain |

- Full CRUD endpoints for terms with search, filter, flagging
- Free Dictionary API integration with concurrent multi-source lookup
- Merriam-Webster API support (when API key provided)
- Auto-seeded database with 25 common business/governance terms

- Dashboard with stats cards and recent terms
- Searchable terms index with status/category filtering
- Term detail page with definitions grouped by source
- Global search page with external dictionary lookup
- Add Term dialog with category selection
- Custom UI components (badge, button, card, dialog, input, select)
- Dark/light theme toggle with system preference detection
- Skeleton loading states, responsive layout
- React Query for data fetching and caching

Infrastructure:
- Docker Compose for local development
- Dockerfiles for both services
- Comprehensive ROADMAP.md with 4-phase plan
- Project README with setup instructions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CoUTE9TPr2udPNrcqwgFss
EOF
)"

content.text:
Everything is committed and pushed. Here's a summary of what was built:

---

## What's Done

### Phase 1 MVP - Built & Pushed

**Backend** (FastAPI + Python):
- **5 data models**: Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement
- **Full REST API**: CRUD for terms, definitions, flagging, dashboard stats
- **Dictionary lookup**: Free Dictionary API integrated (no key needed), Merriam-Webster ready (needs key)
- **25 seeded business terms**: governance, risk, compliance, process, framework, etc.
- **Auto-generated API docs** at `/docs`

**Frontend** (Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind v4):
- **Dashboard**: stat cards (total terms, approved, needs review, custom, definitions, sources) + recent terms list
- **Terms Index**: searchable data table with status/category filtering, "Add Term" dialog
- **Term Detail**: definitions grouped by source with color-coded badges, "Lookup" button to fetch external definitions, flag status dropdown
- **Search**: global search with live matching + external dictionary lookup
- **Polish**: collapsible sidebar, dark/light mode, skeleton loading states, responsive layout, custom shadcn-inspired UI components

**Infrastructure**: Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, comprehensive README

### Roadmap (in `ROADMAP.md`)

| Phase | Focus | Key Features |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **1** (done) | Foundation & MVP | Term CRUD, dictionary lookup, polished UI |
| **2** | LLM Integration | AI-generated consolidated definitions, refinement workflow (open-source LLM) |
| **3** | Import & Advanced Search | CSV/JSON import, full-text search, export, bulk actions |
| **4** | PDF Import & Demo Polish | PDF extraction, glossary detection, onboarding tour, demo mode |

### To Run Locally

```bash
# Backend
cd backend && python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt && uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Frontend (separate terminal)
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev
```

Then open http://localhost:3000.

The Claude Code web environment runs in a container and doesn't expose ports to the user's browser. The localhost links won't work because the container is isolated. There's no port forwarding mechanism available here like VS Code's port forwarding.

For the two issues:

1. **Viewing the app**: Claude Code web doesn't support port forwarding to the user's browser. The app is running inside the container but isn't accessible externally. The user would need to run this locally on their machine or deploy it somewhere to view it.

2. **New repo**: I can't create a new GitHub repo from this environment since the git proxy only authorizes the `reg-scorecard` repo. 

I should be straightforward about these constraints and walk them through what they can do next—they'll need to set up the repo on GitHub themselves, then we can proceed from there.

content.text:
Here's the situation with both requests:

### Viewing the app
Claude Code web runs in an isolated container - **ports aren't exposed to your browser**. The servers are running inside the container, but there's no port forwarding to reach them from your machine. To actually see the app, you have two options:

1. **Run it locally** - Clone the repo to your machine and run the backend + frontend (instructions in the README)
2. **Deploy it** - Push to a platform like Vercel + Railway that gives you a public URL

### Moving to a new repo
The git proxy in this environment only has access to `gavintreseder/reg-scorecard`. I can't create a new repo from here. Here's what you can do:

**Option A** (quickest):
1. Go to https://github.com/new and create a private repo called `data-dictionary`
2. Then on your local machine:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/gavintreseder/reg-scorecard.git data-dictionary
   cd data-dictionary
   git remote set-url origin https://github.com/gavintreseder/data-dictionary.git
   git push -u origin main
   ```

**Option B** - You can also **rename** the existing `reg-scorecard` repo to `data-dictionary` directly in GitHub Settings > General > Repository name.

Would you like me to focus on getting deployment configs set up (e.g., for Vercel + Railway) so you can deploy and get a shareable URL?
Let me check what's in this project and what needs to be run.

file.content:
# DataDict - Data Dictionary Web App

A comprehensive data dictionary management tool that aggregates definitions from multiple sources, supports business-specific terminology, and provides AI-powered definition synthesis.

## Features

- **Multi-source dictionary lookup** - Search terms across Free Dictionary API, Merriam-Webster, and more
- **Business data dictionary** - Define and manage custom business terms with categories and flags
- **Searchable term index** - Browse, filter, and search all terms with status tracking
- **Definition aggregation** - View all definitions for a term grouped by source
- **Flagging system** - Flag terms as approved, needs review, disputed, or archived
- **Dark mode** - Full dark/light theme support
- **Responsive design** - Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Backend | FastAPI, Python 3.12, SQLModel, SQLite |
| State | TanStack React Query |
| UI | Custom components (shadcn/ui-inspired) |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- Node.js 18+
- npm

### Backend

```bash
cd backend
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

The API will be available at http://localhost:8000. Visit http://localhost:8000/docs for the interactive API docs.

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

The app will be available at http://localhost:3000.

### Docker Compose

```bash
docker-compose up
```

Both services will start - frontend at http://localhost:3000, backend at http://localhost:8000.

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
    config.py            # Environment configuration
    database.py          # Database engine and session
    models/term.py       # SQLModel data models
    schemas/term.py      # Pydantic API schemas
    routers/
      terms.py           # Term CRUD + flagging endpoints
      lookup.py          # External dictionary lookup
      sources.py         # Dictionary source management
    services/
      dictionary_service.py  # External API integrations
    seed/
      sample_terms.json  # Initial business terms
  requirements.txt
  Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx           # Root layout with sidebar
    page.tsx             # Dashboard
    terms/page.tsx       # Terms index
    terms/[id]/page.tsx  # Term detail
    search/page.tsx      # Global search + lookup
  components/
    ui/                  # Reusable UI components
    sidebar-nav.tsx      # Navigation sidebar
    add-term-dialog.tsx  # New term form
    flag-badge.tsx       # Status badges
    source-badge.tsx     # Source type badges
  lib/
    api.ts               # API client functions
    types.ts             # TypeScript type definitions
```

## Roadmap

See [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) for the full phased development roadmap.

content.text:
The git proxy in this environment only authorizes access to `gavintreseder/reg-scorecard`. I can't create or push to other repositories from here.

**You'll need to create it yourself** - it takes about 30 seconds:

1. Go to **https://github.com/new**
2. Repository name: `data-dictionary`
3. Set to **Private**
4. Click **Create repository** (don't add README or .gitignore)

Then on your local machine, run:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/gavintreseder/reg-scorecard.git data-dictionary
cd data-dictionary
git remote set-url origin https://github.com/gavintreseder/data-dictionary.git
git push -u origin --all
```

This will copy all branches and history into the new repo. Would you like me to help with anything else in the meantime?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- session-start-hook: Creating and developing startup hooks for Claude Code on the web. Use when the user wants to set up a repository for Claude Code on the web, create a SessionStart hook to ensure their project can run tests and linters during web sessions.

file.content:
# Stage 1: Build frontend
FROM node:20-slim AS frontend
WORKDIR /frontend
COPY frontend/package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY frontend/ .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 2: Python app
FROM python:3.13-slim

WORKDIR /app

# curl needed by start.sh to wait for Streamlit health check
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Python deps
COPY pyproject.toml .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .

# Application code
COPY config.py .
COPY db/ db/
COPY pipeline/ pipeline/
COPY analysis/ analysis/
COPY api/ api/
COPY app/ app/
COPY .streamlit/ .streamlit/
COPY data/seed/ data/seed/
COPY data/samples/ data/samples/

# Built frontend from stage 1
COPY --from=frontend /frontend/dist frontend/dist/

# Startup script that runs both FastAPI and Streamlit
COPY start.sh .
RUN chmod +x start.sh

# Run as non-root user for security
RUN useradd -m -u 1000 appuser && chown -R appuser:appuser /app
USER appuser

EXPOSE ${PORT:-8080}

CMD ["./start.sh"]
